title: sanguisorba ancistroides subsp. parviflora
intro: Sanguisorba ancistroides subsp. parviflora, also known as a type of burnet, is a plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects, which can help to treat wounds, burns, and other skin irritations. The plant's health benefits also include its ability to reduce bleeding, promote healing, and protect against infection. In herbal preparations, Sanguisorba ancistroides subsp. parviflora is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation.
